articles.title: Zelenskyy says Russia fired over 200 drones at Ukraine as truce expires
articles.summary: One killed and four others wounded in attacks on Ukraine's Dnipropetrovsk region, local administration chief says.

articles.title: France’s Macron announces $27bn investment in Africa at Kenya summit
articles.summary: Africa Forward summit comes amid France's fading ties with its former African colonies, as Kenya seeks greater trade.

articles.title: ‘Whole morning revolves around gas’: Pakistan’s fuel crisis enters kitchens
articles.summary: The crisis sees women waking up early, cooking quickly, rearranging meals and planning days around the prospect of gas.

articles.title: Trump and Xi: The history of encounters between two superpower leaders
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping have met six times since 2017.

articles.summary: Gunman arrested after opening fire on busy street near Harvard University

articles.summary: The UK prime minister is so far defiant in the face of calls for him to step down.

articles.title: Mexico cancels plan to cut school year for World Cup after backlash
articles.summary: Officials agree to keep the school calendar as originally planned, ending on July 15 and resuming on August 31.

articles.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Israeli settlers rampage through West Bank villages
articles.summary: Settlers uproot olive trees, seize land, and displace families, reflecting unchecked violence across the West Bank.

articles.title: ‘Potential security risk’: Unpacking the UK’s trust issues with Palantir
articles.summary: Analysts say it is challenging to track whether Palantir is sticking to the terms of its NHS contract.

articles.title: Starving on the front lines: Food supply in crisis as Ukraine fights Russia

articles.title: Why is Israel allowed to take part in the Eurovision song contest?
articles.summary: Five countries are boycotting the 70th edition of Eurovision on May 16 in protest over Israel's genocidal war on Gaza.

articles.summary: Reigning champions Oklahoma City Thunder remain undefeated in playoffs and move on to Western Conference finals.

articles.title: Global energy crisis highlights meagre oil buffers in developing world

articles.summary: US Department of Energy moves to transfer 53.3 million barrels amid rising oil prices.

articles.title: Israel approves law on public trials, death penalty for October 7 detainees

articles.summary: Ex-mayor of wealthy Los Angeles suburb promoted pro-China propaganda at behest of Chinese officials, prosecutors say.

articles.title: Lebanese in south refuse to flee again despite escalating Israeli strikes
articles.summary: Obaida Hitto reports from southern Lebanon, where residents say they will not leave again despite intensifying strikes.
